Title: Genome-wide identification and expression analysis of NtbHLH gene family in Tobacco (Nicotiana tabacum L.) and the role of NtbHLH86 in drought adaptation

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: NtbHLH86 is a member of the tobacco bHLH transcription factor family and was identified as a homologue of NtMYC2, which is related to JA and nicotine pathways; by analogy to AtMYC2, it may be linked to ABA signaling, although ABA responses or downstream target genes were not directly tested in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. NtbHLH86, a tobacco bHLH transcription factor and NtMYC2 homologue, was selected from a genome-wide NtbHLH family analysis.
2. NtbHLH86 transcript levels are induced by drought treatment in tobacco seedlings.
3. Multiple independent NtbHLH86 overexpression lines show significantly higher NtbHLH86 expression confirmed by qRT-PCR.
4. Under drought stress, NtbHLH86 overexpression plants display reduced wilting and a significantly higher survival ratio than wild-type plants, whereas no obvious growth difference is observed under normal watering.
5. These results demonstrate that NtbHLH86 positively regulates drought stress tolerance in cultivated tobacco.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Tobacco gene-chip microarray profiling of NtbHLH family expression across 23 tissues (Bai et al. 2019) was used to assess NtbHLH86 transcript abundance; platform accession numbers were not provided.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of NtbHLH86 in tobacco increases resistance to drought stress, leading to less wilting and higher survival after prolonged water deficit, while showing no obvious growth differences from wild type under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Abiotic stress response pathway, acting as a positive regulator of drought stress tolerance in tobacco.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix–loop–helix (bHLH) transcription factor family member.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: NtbHLH86 is among NtbHLH genes with relatively high expression across tested tissues in microarray data and its transcript level is further induced by drought stress in whole seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by carefully removing plants from pots, washing off surface soil, and air-drying whole seedlings on the bench, and by withholding water from potted plants for 14–20 days.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Stress tolerance and drought adaptation mediated by bHLH transcription factor NtbHLH86 in cultivated tobacco.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: NtbHLH86 overexpression lines exhibit a significantly higher survival ratio than wild-type plants after 20 days of drought treatment, with statistical significance reported (p < 0.05 to p < 0.001), although exact percentage values are not specified in the text.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under normal growth conditions, NtbHLH86 overexpression plants show no significant growth differences compared with wild-type plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Nicotiana tabacum cv. Yunyan87 wild-type plants and multiple independent NtbHLH86 overexpression lines; soil-grown seedlings (6–7 leaves) in pots used for drought treatment assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of NtbHLH genes using HMMER against the China tobacco genome database V2.0; cloning of full-length NtbHLH86 CDS from tobacco leaf cDNA into pDONR-zeo and pB2GW7 vectors; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate overexpression lines; drought treatment by air-drying whole seedlings after removing soil from roots and by withholding water from potted plants; qRT-PCR to measure NtbHLH86 expression under drought and in transgenic lines; phenotypic observation of wilting after 14 days of drought and calculation of seedling survival ratio after 20 days.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression of NtbHLH86 in tobacco (transgenic lines generated via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation) combined with drought-stress assays and qRT-PCR analysis of NtbHLH86 induction by drought and transgene expression levels.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of a genome-wide NtbHLH family scan based on the bHLH domain using HMMER and selected as a NtMYC2 homologue; full-length CDS of NtbHLH86 was amplified from tobacco leaf cDNA and cloned into Gateway vectors for overexpression.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of NtbHLH86 significantly enhances drought tolerance in cultivated tobacco without penalizing growth under normal watering, suggesting NtbHLH86 is a promising candidate gene for breeding or engineering drought-tolerant tobacco cultivars to improve performance under water-limited conditions.
